name: cover
voice: the live magazine-cover takeover — the transcript reads as a small editorial column docked in a clear corner (folio numbers, hairline rules, a barcode bug; words INK-SETTLE in, the emphasis word flips to an italic swash, lines exit on a page-turn). At the apex the room becomes paper: a serif masthead of the hero word SETS ACROSS THE TOP of frame behind the subject's head (classic cover occlusion), a kicker + issue date + lipstick-red accent bar deploy around it, and the cover breathes — print never freezes.
when: editorial, fashion/culture/lifestyle, premium brand and founder profiles, thought-leadership and longform — anywhere the speaker should read like a cover story. Calm-elegant register; needs a bright top (the scrim turns sky into paper) and a clear lower corner for the column.
